summaAs .a researcher, it is important to understand the role of parenting in the development of children and their behavioral patterns. Comprehensive Sexuality Education (CSE) has gained strong international support and is based on a solid evidence base. Parents can play an important role in providing CSE to their children, as it is easier to begin the conversation about sexuality at a young age and build upon it over time. One of the main responsibilities of parents is to protect their children from sexual abuse, and building self-esteem in children can be an effective factor in this regard. Parents are encouraged to value their children and be available to truthfully answer their difficult questions.
As a researcher, it is important to recognize the role of media in providing Comprehensive Sexuality Education (CSE) to youth. While some young people rely on media for information related to sex, it is important to note that only a limited number of youth television series actually address the risks and responsibilities associated with sex, including the use of contraceptives, pregnancy, and sexually transmitted infections. Researchers have theorized that media can function as a “super-peer,” influencing teens’ behaviors, romantic interactions, and decisions related to sexual activity. As such, media literacy education may be a promising approach to teaching adolescents CSE.rize it please in best and perfect way.

As a researcher, it is essential to understand the important role that parents play in providing Comprehensive Sexuality Education (CSE) to their children, as well as protecting them from sexual abuse and building their self-esteem. It is also critical to recognize the influence that media can have on young people and their behaviors related to sexuality, and the potential for media literacy education to support CSE efforts. Taking a comprehensive approach to both parental involvement and media literacy education can help ensure that young people have access to the accurate and relevant information and skills they need to make healthy and informed decisions about their sexual health.

Hello AI summarize the under litAs a professional researcher, it is important to consider the ethical practices surrounding the portrayal of child sexual abuse on electronic media. The Pakistan Coalition for Ethical Journalism (PCEJ) has gained attention in the media for their efforts to address this issue, particularly in relation to the Kasur Incident in 2015, which highlighted the sexual abuse of many boys over an extended period of time. While the media has played an essential role in finding and punishing those responsible for such incidents, there is a concern that sensationalized coverage can further harm innocent children who are victims of abuse, particularly if their cases are not adequately covered. PCEJ advocates for best practices in ethical and professional journalism, including protecting the privacy of victims and their families, reporting sensitively, using clear distinctions between facts and opinions, providing a broader perspective, regular follow-up, and promoting education and reform to prevent child sexual abuse. It is the responsibility of media practitioners and professionals to act as guardians for the public interest and adhere to these ethical practices.
As a professional researcher, it is important to understand the news media’s role in reporting on child sexual abuse cases. In 2015, a gang of criminals involved in child abuse in Kasur, Pakistan was brought to light. The gang had been involved in this criminal activity for 10 years and was also involved in child pornography, with over 25 members in their gang. The media coverage of the incident was extensive and provided sufficient information regarding the scandal. Even though the police’s statements were inconsistent, the media took responsibility for reporting on the incident. The scandal was the largest in Pakistan’s history, and the media gave it special attention as well as publishing awareness articles. The media’s reporting on such incidents is crucial not just to bring the perpetrators to justice, but to raise public awareness and prevent future abuse.erature in perfect way for research paper.

As a professional researcher, ethically reporting on child sexual abuse in electronic media is important to consider. The Pakistan Coalition for Ethical Journalism (PCEJ) is addressing these concerns following the Kasur Incident in 2015, where children were sexually abused over an extended period. While media plays an essential role in finding and punishing responsible individuals, sensationalized coverage risks further harm to victims if their cases are not adequately reported. PCEJ advocates for best practices in ethical journalism, including protecting victim privacy, reporting sensitively, distinguishing facts from opinion, providing context, regular follow-up, and supporting preventative education and reform. Researchers must recognize the media's role in reporting on child sexual abuse, like the Kasur scandal, to increase public awareness and prevent future abuse.

summarize The statement discusses the portrayal of child sexual abuse on the Pakistani television series “Udaari”, which aired from April to September 2016 on the channel Hum TV. The series was co-produced by Momina Duraid and Kashaf Foundation and was written by Farhat Ishtiaq, with direction by Muhammad Ehteshamuddin. The plot focused on the marginalized segments of society and highlighted social issues such as child sexual abuse, parental retaliation against music careers, police interrogations, and blind support of politicians. The series broke stereotypes and taboos surrounding these issues to help find solutions..

This statement highlights the Pakistani television series "Udaari," which aired in 2016 and focused on social issues such as child sexual abuse, parental retaliation against music careers, police interrogations, and blind support of politicians. The series was co-produced by Momina Duraid and Kashaf Foundation, written by Farhat Ishtiaq, and directed by Muhammad Ehteshamuddin. The show broke stereotypes and taboo around these issues to raise awareness and find solutions.

convert this literature into The television series “Udaari” became a topic of discussion across Pakistan when it took a bold and unusual turn in the 6th episode by revealing Imtiaz’s bad intentions towards his stepdaughter, Zebo, and showcasing the issue of child sexual abuse. The series had a significant impact on viewers, highlighting the bitter realities of society and raising awareness about the need to protect children from sexual abuse. However, because families are the primary viewers of prime-time television, traditional themes are widely accepted, and some viewers lodged complaints with PEMRA about the portrayal of child sexual abuse, resulting in a “show cause” notice to HUM TV, which was later rescinded due to social media backlash.
The TV series “Udaari” tackled difficult issues in society, including victim shaming in cases of sexual abuse. The show encouraged victims to not be ashamed and assured them that they are survivors, not victims. The series also paired the concepts of parenting and women’s empowerment, providing a positive message and hope for survivors of child abuse. Overall, the show raised awareness of important social issues and broke new ground in Pakistani television.
Research has established that heavy and light TV viewing can have a significant impact on viewers, affecting their beliefs, perceptions, and patterns of social interaction. Demographic and personality characteristics of viewers can play a role in driving heavy or light viewing, with corresponding impacts on knowledge, attitude, and practices regarding certain issues. Exposure to violence on prime TV dramas has been linked to the cultivation of fear among viewers, while dysphoria - or feelings of unease and dissatisfaction - has been reported as a result of heavy TV viewing.
Research suggests that the media’s focus on violent and sensational crimes can cultivate an overstated fear of crime in the public. Domestic and child abuse are often not reported, making it difficult to address these issues. Media reports often follow atypical patterns, portraying rape as being committed by strangers lurking in the night, when in reality many rapes are committed by familiar people. Women are at a higher risk of being abused and raped within their own homes.

Cultivation theory explains that heavy TV viewing leads to a belief that the world is dangerous, called the Mean World Syndrome. Heavy viewers are vulnerable to media messages and believe them to be accurate representations of reality. Researchers focus on the impact of TV on viewers' attitudes rather than behavior. Cultivation theory suggests that media reflects existing attitudes in society and can shape viewers' perspectives on violence. The theory argues that TV does not create anything new and that viewers are often unaware of how much media they consume. Overall, media has a small but significant impact on society, especially on heavy viewers.

Cultivation theory explains that heavy TV viewers can be influenced to believe that the world is more dangerous than it really is due to the violence portrayed on TV, resulting in the Mean World Syndrome. The theory suggests that TV viewing can have long-term impacts on viewers and shapes their perspectives on the world. Researchers focus on viewer attitudes rather than their behavior and suggest that heavy viewers can create a distorted perception of reality based on what they see on TV. The theory also distinguishes between general beliefs about existence and specific attitudes towards topics such as peace or pedophilia.

One of the essential statutes of the theory is that TV and media create nothing new, they don't move it. The theory suggests that TV and media have a little yet vital effect on the perspectives and feelings of society about society. The people who ingest more media are those we are more affected.

Cultivation theory suggests that media shapes attitudes based on perspectives already present in society, as the media re-presents them to viewers. The theory emphasizes that TV and media do not create anything new, but rather package existing perspectives. Viewers may not realize how much media they consume, often assuming they are light viewers when they are actually heavy viewers. The theory concludes that while media has only a small effect on society's perspectives and feelings about itself, heavy media consumers are the most affected.

summarize  KAP Model
KAP refers to knowledge, attitude and practices. It is a method which includes standardized questionnaires which result in quantitative data. These surveys show misunderstandings that signify difficulties regarding activities that should be implemented. Potential hurdles to change behavior are also included. This survey discloses the things that are said, but there are huge gaps between the things being said and the things being done.[ The KAP survey Model (Knowledge, Attitude and Practices) Accessed from https://www.spring-nutrition.org/publications/tool-summaries/kap-survey-model-knowledge-attitudes-and-practices  Accessed on April 15, 2017]

Research Methodology 
13.1. Research Design
For the research in hand, the researcher has made use of both qualitative and quantitative tools of data collection aimed at triangulation and generalizability of the results. Further, the data was collected through PAPI[ PAPI is an abbreviation of Paper and Pen Interview. This is traditional method of data collection. ] and CAPI[ CAPI is an abbreviation of Computer-Assisted Personal Interviewing. This method saves time of time of researches in terms of data entry and analysis. ] as methods of data collection where, quantitative data was collected through CAPI and qualitative data was collected through PAPI.

The KAP model, which stands for knowledge, attitude, and practices, involves standardized questionnaires to gather quantitative data and identify obstacles to implementing desired behaviors. Though the survey may reveal discrepancies between what people say and what they actually do. The research design in this study used both qualitative and quantitative tools and collected data through paper-and-pen interviews (PAPI) and computer-assisted personal interviews (CAPI).

summarize . Under this study, various strata were demarcated and sample was distributed in those strata by using Quasi-Experimental Design of treatment and controlled group so that, difference in knowledge, attitude and practices of the parents and key stakeholders towards the portrayal of child sexual abuse through the lens of gender on electronic media could be gauged among the viewers and non-viewers of Hum TV and private news channels of Pakistan.
